description: Pause your task and ask the user a question. Use when you genuinely need user input to proceed -- e.g. ambiguous requirements, a critical design choice, or needing credentials/paths you can't infer. Keep it rare: ask at most once per task.
name: ask_user

options.description: Optional JSON array of suggested answer strings, e.g. ["Option A","Option B"]. Omit if open-ended.

question.description: Clear, specific question to ask the user.

description: Fetch and extract readable content from a URL. Use this to deep-read an article, documentation page, or any web page after finding it via web_search. Returns clean text and links.
name: fetch_page

mode.description: 'text' for article body text (default), or 'links' to list all hyperlinks on the page.

url.description: The full URL to fetch (must start with http:// or https://).

description: Fetch a public URL and return its text content (HTML stripped). Use for documentation, GitHub issues, Stack Overflow answers, changelogs. Max 8000 chars returned. Do NOT use for internal/private URLs.
name: fetch_url

url.description: The public URL to fetch (https:// only)

description: Get a structural outline of a file (classes, methods, functions).
name: get_outline

description: Search code for a pattern. Uses ripgrep if available, falls back to built-in.
name: grep_code

path.description: Directory to search. Defaults to workspace root.

description: Inspect provisional Context Fabric claims, entities, and relations. Read-only.
name: library_graph

entity_id.description: Optional entity id filter for relations.

description: List Context Fabric corpora and document counts. Read-only.
name: library_list

description: Open exact Context Fabric source segments by document_id or segment_id. Read-only.
name: library_open

count.description: When opening a document, max segment count. Default 8.

start_ordinal.description: When opening a document, first ordinal to include. Default 0.

description: Search Context Fabric segment text by BM25/FTS and return source-grounded hits. Read-only.
name: library_search

description: List files in a directory (recursive, respects .gitignore-style skips).
name: list_files

path.description: Directory path. Defaults to workspace root.

description: Read the contents of a file.
name: read_file

path.description: File path relative to workspace root, or absolute.

description: Run a PowerShell command in the workspace. Use env_setup to source an environment script BEFORE the command -- both run in the same process so variables like IDF_PATH survive. Example: env_setup=". C:\esp-idf\export.ps1", command="idf.py build". Blocked: destructive commands.
name: run_shell

env_setup.description: Optional. A PowerShell snippet run BEFORE command in the same process. Use this to source environment scripts (e.g. ". C:\esp-idf\export.ps1"). The environment it sets is visible to command.

reason.description: Why this command needs to run.

description: Two-stage verify: (1) build/compile check always runs, (2) test suite runs only if test files are found. Auto-detects: dotnet, pytest, npm/tsc, cargo, go.
name: run_tests

path.description: Path to verify. Defaults to workspace root.

description: Save a markdown document into the current workspace and return its path.
name: save_markdown_document

content.description: Full markdown document body to save.

filename.description: File name, with or without the .md extension.

folder.description: Optional workspace-relative folder. Defaults to chat-output.

description: Search the web using DuckDuckGo. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets for the most relevant results. Use this first when you need current information or facts you're not certain about.
name: web_search

max_results.description: How many results to return. Default: 6, max: 10.

query.description: The search query. Be specific. Use quotes around phrases.

description: Write content to a file. Shows a diff preview before writing.
name: write_file

path.description: File path relative to workspace root.

reason.description: Why this change is being made.
